span.p: GUI shape — one row per display

span.p:
group key derived from groupOf
→ name lookup

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
position 1 → Group A
position 2 → Group A
position 3 → — None
position 4 → Group B
Letters are derived display labels
(sort order). The persisted IDs are
opaque — see S2.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Conversion is one-way at runtime:
JSON is canonical, engine is derived.
list-of-lists never mutated by hand.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Single-group constraint is structural:
groupOf is a map keyed by position.
A position can have ONE group ID.
Cannot represent overlap by construction.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Why opaque group IDs (grp_xxxxxx) instead of letters?
Letters are unstable: reshuffling groups in the GUI
can relabel A→B. When groups gain metadata in v2
(custom name, color, per-group switchDelay), letter
keys would associate metadata with the wrong group.
Opaque IDs survive any GUI reshuffle.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
hs.settings: SpacesSync.spaceNames
{
"1,2": { 1: "Notes", 2: "Music" },
"4": { 1: "Email" }
}
Keyed by sorted group positions.
Not migrated to JSON in v1.
＊Migration contract on groupOf change＊
1. compute old → new key map
2. rewrite spaceNames keys atomically
3. orphans (key has no current group)
surface as a warning, not silent loss
